perturbation.original_text: Such amounts shall be billed on a bi-monthly basis by Equidata and are due and payable in full by Marketing Affiliate 30 days from the invoice date. The prices set forth in Exhibit A do not include regulatory fees, sales tax, excise tax or any other fees or taxes that may be charged by states or local taxing authorities nor does it include additional fees or surcharges, including specific area Affiliate charges that may be accessed by the Credit Reporting Agencies (CRA's). Said amounts charged to Equidata will be billed separately to Marketing Affiliate and are due immediately upon receipt. Marketing Affiliate agrees to reimburse Equidata all costs of collecting any past due amounts from Marketing Affiliate by reason of non payment, including reasonable attorney fees and disbursements. Equidata reserves the right to increase the base cost of Services. Notice will be given to Marketing Affiliate in writing no less than 30 days prior to such increase taking affect. A development fee, yet To Be Determined and outlined in Exhibit A, is due upon a signed agreement of project scope. Marketing Affiliate agrees to pay promptly and in full all charges incurred through services rendered when billed.
perturbation.changed_text: Such amounts shall be billed on a bi-monthly basis by Equidata and are due and payable in full by Marketing Affiliate 30 days from the invoice date. Marketing Affiliate agrees to reimburse Equidata all costs of collecting any past due amounts from Marketing Affiliate by reason of non payment, including reasonable attorney fees and disbursements. Equidata reserves the right to increase the base cost of Services. Notice will be given to Marketing Affiliate in writing no less than 30 days prior to such increase taking affect. A development fee is due upon a signed agreement of project scope. Marketing Affiliate agrees to pay promptly and in full all charges incurred through services rendered when billed.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'The prices set forth in Exhibit A do not include regulatory fees, sales tax, excise tax or any other fees or taxes that may be charged by states or local taxing authorities nor does it include additional fees or surcharges, including specific area Affiliate charges that may be accessed by the Credit Reporting Agencies (CRA's). Said amounts charged to Equidata will be billed separately to Marketing Affiliate and are due immediately upon receipt.', the text creates an in-text contradiction, because those ammounts now are part of the exhibit A. The omission creates uncertainty on what is being charged, and the payment term. This contradicts the clarity required in financial agreements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Compensation. Marketing Affiliate shall be responsible for collecting all amounts due directly from the Consumer and shall bear sole responsibility for non-payment of any fees charged to the Consumer. Marketing Affiliate shall pay to Equidata, as compensation for its providing of Services under this agreement, such amounts as outlined and detailed in Exhibit A attached hereto. Such amounts shall be billed on a bi-monthly basis by Equidata and are due and payable in full by Marketing Affiliate 30 days from the invoice date. Marketing Affiliate agrees to reimburse Equidata all costs of collecting any past due amounts from Marketing Affiliate by reason of non payment, including reasonable attorney fees and disbursements. Equidata reserves the right to increase the base cost of Services. Notice will be given to Marketing Affiliate in writing no less than 30 days prior to such increase taking affect. A development fee is due upon a signed agreement of project scope. Marketing Affiliate agrees to pay promptly and in full all charges incurred through services rendered when billed.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly excluded certain fees and taxes from the amounts detailed in Exhibit A, specifying they would be billed separately and due immediately. The changed text removes this exclusion, implying that all fees, including regulatory fees, sales tax, excise tax, and other charges accessed by Credit Reporting Agencies (CRA's), are now included in Exhibit A and subject to the 30-day payment term. This directly contradicts the portion of the original and contradicted text that mandates certain amounts charged to Equidata will be billed separately to Marketing Affiliate and are due immediately upon receipt. The omission creates uncertainty on what is being charged, and the payment term, therefore it introduces an in-text contradiction, because those amounts now are part of the exhibit A. This aligns with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition, creating an ambiguity in the payment terms.

perturbation.original_text: Marketing Affiliate and the undersigned principal, partner or owner further agree that this Agreement will serve as a personal guaranty by the undersigned principal, partner or owner of the company, and the undersigned principal, partner or owner will become responsible for any unpaid balance past due on any invoice. The Marketing Affiliate agrees to pay a late charge of 1 l/2% per month on the unpaid, past-due amount as well as a returned check fee of not less than $35.00 per returned item. In addition, the Marketing Affiliate agrees to pay 25% attorney's fees plus court cost in the event that the Marketing Affiliate's account is referred to an attorney for collection. 2. Disputes. In the case of disputed charge, defined as a non-payment of an invoice for which notice of dispute has been given in writing by Marketing Affiliate to Equidata, Equidata or Marketing Affiliate may choose arbitration and Marketing Affiliate and Equidata shall be obligated by the terms agreed upon by arbitration and all monies determined owed shall be considered due and payable immediately.
perturbation.changed_text: Marketing Affiliate and the undersigned principal, partner or owner further agree that this Agreement will serve as a personal guaranty by the undersigned principal, partner or owner of the company, and the undersigned principal, partner or owner will become responsible for any unpaid balance past due on any invoice. The Marketing Affiliate agrees to pay a late charge on the unpaid, past-due amount as well as a returned check fee of not less than $35.00 per returned item. In addition, the Marketing Affiliate agrees to pay attorney's fees plus court cost in the event that the Marketing Affiliate's account is referred to an attorney for collection. 2. Disputes. In the case of disputed charge, defined as a non-payment of an invoice for which notice of dispute has been given in writing by Marketing Affiliate to Equidata, Equidata or Marketing Affiliate may choose arbitration and Marketing Affiliate and Equidata shall be obligated by the terms agreed upon by arbitration and all monies determined owed shall be considered due and payable immediately.
perturbation.explanation: Removing '1 1/2% per month' and '25%' makes the late charge and attorney's fees undefined. The text still mentions these charges, but the omission creates uncertainty in how they will be calculated. This introduces an in-text contradiction because the text refers to paying these amounts without specifying what they are, potentially making them unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Compensation. Marketing Affiliate shall be responsible for collecting all amounts due directly from the Consumer and shall bear sole responsibility for non-payment of any fees charged to the Consumer. Marketing Affiliate shall pay to Equidata, as compensation for its providing of Services under this agreement, such amounts as outlined and detailed in Exhibit A attached hereto. Such amounts shall be billed on a bi-monthly basis by Equidata and are due and payable in full by Marketing Affiliate 30 days from the invoice date. The prices set forth in Exhibit A do not include regulatory fees, sales tax, excise tax or any other fees or taxes that may be charged by states or local taxing authorities nor does it include additional fees or surcharges, including specific area Affiliate charges that may be accessed by the Credit Reporting Agencies (CRA's). Said amounts charged to Equidata will be billed separately to Marketing Affiliate and are due immediately upon receipt. Marketing Affiliate agrees to reimburse Equidata all costs of collecting any past due amounts from Marketing Affiliate by reason of non payment, including reasonable attorney fees and disbursements. Equidata reserves the right to increase the base cost of Services. Notice will be given to Marketing Affiliate in writing no less than 30 days prior to such increase taking affect. A development fee, yet To Be Determined and outlined in Exhibit A, is due upon a signed agreement of project scope. Marketing Affiliate agrees to pay promptly and in full all charges incurred through services rendered when billed. When paying by credit card, the Marketing Affiliate agrees to pay for all items that are revoked or disputed by the credit card company or the card holder along with any charges or fees charged by the credit card company including fees associated with processing the credit card transaction and that the Marketing Affiliate will be billed for those items in accordance with Equidata standard practices. Marketing Affiliate and the undersigned principal, partner or owner further agree that this Agreement will serve as a personal guaranty by the undersigned principal, partner or owner of the company, and the undersigned principal, partner or owner will become responsible for any unpaid balance past due on any invoice. The Marketing Affiliate agrees to pay a late charge on the unpaid, past-due amount as well as a returned check fee of not less than $35.00 per returned item. In addition, the Marketing Affiliate agrees to pay attorney's fees plus court cost in the event that the Marketing Affiliate's account is referred to an attorney for collection.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific percentages for the late charge and attorney's fees, creating uncertainty and potential unenforceability. The contradicted text, which contains the modified text, refers to these charges, thus creating an in-text contradiction. The original text had '1 l/2% per month' and '25%', the changed text omits these specific values. This omission directly contradicts the implicit requirement for defined charges, as the marketing affiliate is still agreeing to pay these fees, but the exact amount is no longer specified. This falls under 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' because essential language defining the monetary penalty was removed, creating a contradiction with the agreement to pay such charges.

perturbation.original_text: Equidata reserves the right to site inspect Marketing Affiliate's physical location of business at any time. 7. Term and Termination. This Agreement commences on the Effective Date, and terminates, along with all licenses and authorizations granted under it, upon the earliest of termination in accordance with the following. 7.1. This Agreement shall be for the term of one year; thereafter, the Agreement shall renew automatically under these same terms and agreements unless superceded by future agreements. 7.2. This Agreement may be terminated by either party with cause upon thirty (30) days written notice. Upon Marketing Affiliate's default in payment or other breach of this Agreement, Equidata may terminate this Agreement without notice to Marketing Affiliate. Upon termination for any reason, Equidata reserves the right to deactivate Marketing Affiliate's access to the services including the Equidata Web Site. Termination does not release Marketing Affiliate from paying all amounts owed to Equidata. 7.3. At time of Agreement termination, Marketing Affiliate shall immediately remove all URL related data pertaining to said Agreement; and if data is not voluntarily removed, Equidata reserves the right to use all available legal resources to force the removal of Equidata URL related data and Marketing Affiliate agrees to be liable for the cost of such action, including but not limited to reasonable attorney fees. 7.4. Equidata reserves the right to terminate this Agreement immediately for cause if Experian, Equifax and/or TransUnion (Credit Reporting Agencies — CRAs) decline to render Services to Marketing Affiliate for any reason or if Equidata is notified by any of the CRAs to cease rendering Services to Marketing Affiliate.
perturbation.changed_text: Equidata reserves the right to site inspect Marketing Affiliate's physical location of business at any time. 7. Term and Termination. This Agreement commences on the Effective Date, and terminates, along with all licenses and authorizations granted under it, upon the earliest of termination in accordance with the following. 7.1. This Agreement shall be for the term of one year; thereafter, the Agreement shall renew automatically under these same terms and agreements unless superceded by future agreements. 7.2. This Agreement may be terminated by either party with cause upon thirty (30) days written notice. Upon Marketing Affiliate's default in payment or other breach of this Agreement, Equidata may terminate this Agreement without notice to Marketing Affiliate. Upon termination for any reason, Equidata reserves the right to deactivate Marketing Affiliate's access to the services including the Equidata Web Site. Termination does not release Marketing Affiliate from paying all amounts owed to Equidata. 7.3. At time of Agreement termination, Marketing Affiliate shall immediately remove all URL related data pertaining to said Agreement; and if data is not voluntarily removed, Equidata reserves the right to use all available legal resources to force the removal of Equidata URL related data and Marketing Affiliate agrees to be liable for the cost of such action. 7.4. Equidata reserves the right to terminate this Agreement immediately for cause if Experian, Equifax and/or TransUnion (Credit Reporting Agencies — CRAs) decline to render Services to Marketing Affiliate for any reason or if Equidata is notified by any of the CRAs to cease rendering Services to Marketing Affiliate.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'including but not limited to reasonable attorney fees' from 7.3 creates an in-text contradiction. The text in 7.3 requires the Marketing Affiliate to be liable for the costs of forcing the removal of Equidata URL-related data, but it no longer specifies that attorney's fees are included in these costs. This affects the clarity and enforceability of cost recovery in case of data removal enforcement, as the original clause states reasonable attorney fees
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term and Termination. This Agreement commences on the Effective Date, and terminates, along with all licenses and authorizations granted under it, upon the earliest of termination in accordance with the following. 7.1. This Agreement shall be for the term of one year; thereafter, the Agreement shall renew automatically under these same terms and agreements unless superceded by future agreements. 7.2. This Agreement may be terminated by either party with cause upon thirty (30) days written notice. Upon Marketing Affiliate's default in payment or other breach of this Agreement, Equidata may terminate this Agreement without notice to Marketing Affiliate. Upon termination for any reason, Equidata reserves the right to deactivate Marketing Affiliate's access to the services including the Equidata Web Site. Termination does not release Marketing Affiliate from paying all amounts owed to Equidata. 7.3. At time of Agreement termination, Marketing Affiliate shall immediately remove all URL related data pertaining to said Agreement; and if data is not voluntarily removed, Equidata reserves the right to use all available legal resources to force the removal of Equidata URL related data and Marketing Affiliate agrees to be liable for the cost of such action. 7.4. Equidata reserves the right to terminate this Agreement immediately for cause if Experian, Equifax and/or TransUnion (Credit Reporting Agencies — CRAs) decline to render Services to Marketing Affiliate for any reason or if Equidata is notified by any of the CRAs to cease rendering Services to Marketing Affiliate.
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'including but not limited to reasonable attorney fees' from section 7.3 creates an in-text contradiction by making the cost recovery in case of data removal enforcement unclear. The original clause clearly states that reasonable attorney fees are included in the costs for which the Marketing Affiliate is liable. The modified text in the changed section 7.3 does not specify whether attorney's fees are included. The change therefore creates uncertainty, inconsistency, and potentially affects enforceability, breaking compatibility with the original intention.
